2604.15723.title: Diffusion Autoencoder for Unsupervised Artifact Restoration in Handheld Fundus Images
2604.15723.paper_summary: The advent of handheld fundus imaging devices has made ophthalmologic diagnosis and disease screening more accessible, efficient, and cost-effective. However, images captured from these setups often suffer from artifacts such as flash reflections, exposure variations, and motion-induced blur, which degrade image quality and hinder downstream analysis. While generative models have been effective in image restoration, most depend on paired supervision or predefined artifact structures, making them less adaptable to unstructured degradations commonly observed in handheld fundus images. To address this, we propose an unsupervised diffusion autoencoder that integrates a context encoder with the denoising process to learn semantically meaningful representations for artifact restoration. The model is trained only on high-quality table-top fundus images and infers to restore artifact-affected handheld acquisitions. We validate the restorations through quantitative and qualitative evaluations, and have shown that diagnostic accuracy increases to 81.17% on an unseen dataset and multiple artifact conditions
2604.15723.paper_summary_zh: 手持式眼底成像設備的出現使得眼科診斷和疾病篩查變得更加可及、高效和具成本效益。然而，這些設備捕捉的圖像經常受到閃光反射、曝光變化和運動引起的模糊等伪影的影響，這些都降低了圖像質量並妨礙了後續分析。雖然生成模型在圖像修復方面已經取得了有效的成果，但大多數依賴於配對監督或預定義的伪影結構，使其對於手持眼底圖像中常見的非結構性降解適應性較差。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種無監督的擴散自編碼器，該編碼器將上下文編碼器與去噪過程整合，以學習對伪影修復具有語義意義的表示。該模型僅在高質量的桌面眼底圖像上進行訓練，並推斷以修復受伪影影響的手持圖像。我們通過定量和定性評估驗證了修復效果，並顯示在未見過的數據集和多種伪影條件下，診斷準確率提高至81.17%。
2604.15723.author: Mathumetha Palani et.al.
2604.15723.authors: Mathumetha Palani, Kavya Puthumana, Ayantika Das, Ganapathy Krishnamurthi
